title: Pleural Fluid Analysis: Methodology and Equipment Updates
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9lcoi","text":" This protocol outlines the methodologies employed for pleural fluid analysis in the laboratory, detailing both historical and current practices. Since 2014, cell and differential counts have been performed using flow cytometry on the Sysmex XN-1000 analyzer, transitioning from manual counting with a Neubauer chamber and panoptic staining. Adenosine deaminase (ADA) levels are measured using an automated UV kinetic assay at 37°C, tracking NADH depletion, with updates in 2024 aligning calibrator values to BCR-647 reference material. Biochemical parameters such as LDH, glucose, and total proteins are analyzed on Cobas 501/502 analyzers using spectrophotometric and enzymatic methods, with a platform shift to Cobas 303 in 2022 that preserved method consistency. Pleural pH has been measured using IL GEM gasometers, transitioning from the Premier 4000 to the IL GEM 5000 in April 2025. These protocols ensure accurate, standardized evaluation of pleural fluid for diagnostic purposes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Since 2014, cell and differential counts were performed by flow cytometry in an analyzer Sysmex XN-1000 (Roche Diagnostics). Before this period, a manual counting at optical microscope was made using the Neubauer Improved chamber, followed by extension and panoptic staining for differential count.

Pleural fluid ADA level was measured using an automated ultraviolet kinetic assay at 37º C based on NADH depletion rate determination at 340 nm in a Cobas 501 or 502 analyzer (Roche Diagnostics), with BioSystems's reactive (Adenosine deaminase hydrolyzes adenosine to form inosine and ammonia that reacts with 2-oxoglutarate and NADH).
Pleural LDH, Glucose, and Total Proteins were measured also in Cobas 501 or 502 analyzer (Roche Diagnostics) with the following methods:
LDH was measured by a kinetic spectrophotometry method based on NADH production as a result of reaction from lactate to pyruvate, N-methylglucamine as buffer.
Total Proteins were measured by a Biuret final point and Glucose by Hexokinase method.
At last, pleural pH was performed with the gasometer IL GEM Premier 4000 (Werfen).
Since December 2021/January 2022, Cobas 501 analyzer is replaced by Cobas 303 but methods didn't change.

In May 2024, there was a change for ADA. The base of the method was the same, but the calibrator value is traceable to the reference material BCR-647 (IRMM) (Determination in a Cobas 303 with BioSystem's reactive).
Finally, in April 2025, the gasometer IL GEM 4000 (Werfen) was replaced by IL GEM 5000 (Werfen) for pleural pH measurement.
creator: Rafael Arnay
